NKJV.1: Wisdom has built her house, She has hewn out her seven pillars;
NKJV.2: She has slaughtered her meat,  She has mixed her wine, She has also furnished her table.
NKJV.3: She has sent out her maidens, She cries out from the highest places of the city,
NKJV.4: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” As for him who lacks understanding, she says to him,
NKJV.5: “Come,  eat of my bread And drink of the wine I have mixed.
NKJV.6: Forsake foolishness and live, And go in the way of understanding.
NKJV.7: “He who corrects a scoffer gets shame for himself, And he who rebukes a wicked man only harms himself.
NKJV.8: Do not correct a scoffer, lest he hate you; Rebuke a wise man, and he will love you.
NKJV.9: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be still wiser; Teach a just man, and he will increase in learning.
NKJV.10: “The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, And the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
NKJV.11: For by me your days will be multiplied, And years of life will be added to you.
NKJV.12: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself, And if you scoff, you will bear it alone.”
NKJV.13: A foolish woman is clamorous; She is simple, and knows nothing.
NKJV.14: For she sits at the door of her house, On a seat by the highest places of the city,
NKJV.15: To call to those who pass by, Who go straight on their way:
NKJV.16: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here”; And as for him who lacks understanding, she says to him,
NKJV.17: “Stolen water is sweet, And bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
NKJV.18: But he does not know that the dead are there, That her guests are in the depths of hell.

KJV.8: Reprove<S>3198</S> not a scorner<S>3887</S>, lest he hate<S>8130</S> thee: rebuke<S>3198</S> a wise man<S>2450</S>, and he will love<S>157</S> thee.
KJV.9: Give<S>5414</S> instruction to a wise<S>2450</S> man, and he will be yet wiser<S>2449</S>: teach<S>3045</S> a just<S>6662</S> man, and he will increase<S>3254</S> in learning<S>3948</S>.

KJV.12: If thou be wise<S>2449</S>, thou shalt be wise<S>2449</S> for thyself: but if thou scornest<S>3887</S>, thou alone shalt bear<S>5375</S> it.
KJV.13: A foolish<S>3687</S> woman<S>802</S> is clamorous<S>1993</S>: she is simple<S>6615</S>, and<S>1077</S> knoweth<S>3045</S> nothing<S>4100</S>.
KJV.14: For she sitteth<S>3427</S> at the door<S>6607</S> of her house<S>1004</S>, on a seat<S>3678</S> in the high places<S>4791</S> of the city<S>7176</S>,
KJV.15: To call<S>7121</S> passengers<S>5674</S> <S>1870</S> who go right<S>3474</S> on their ways<S>734</S>:

KJV.18: But he knoweth<S>3045</S> not that the dead<S>7496</S> are there; and that her guests<S>7121</S> are in the depths<S>6012</S> of hell<S>7585</S>.

ESV.1: Wisdom has built her house; she has hewn her seven pillars.
ESV.2: She has slaughtered her beasts; she has mixed her wine; she has also set her table.
ESV.3: She has sent out her young women to call from the highest places in the town,
ESV.4: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” To him who lacks sense she says,
ESV.5: “Come, eat of my bread and drink of the wine I have mixed.
ESV.6: Leave your simple ways,  and live, and walk in the way of insight.”
ESV.7: Whoever corrects a scoffer gets himself abuse, and he who reproves a wicked man incurs injury.
ESV.8: Do not reprove a scoffer, or he will hate you; reprove a wise man, and he will love you.
ESV.9: Give instruction  to a wise man, and he will be still wiser; teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning.
ESV.10: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight.
ESV.11: For by me your days will be multiplied, and years will be added to your life.
ESV.12: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself; if you scoff, you alone will bear it.
ESV.13: The woman Folly is loud; she is seductive  and knows nothing.
ESV.14: She sits at the door of her house; she takes a seat on the highest places of the town,
ESV.15: calling to those who pass by, who are going straight on their way,
ESV.16: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” And to him who lacks sense she says,
ESV.17: “Stolen water is sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
ESV.18: But he does not know that the dead  are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

NASB.1: Wisdom has built her house, She has hewn out her seven pillars;
NASB.2: She has prepared her food, she has mixed her wine; She has also set her table;
NASB.3: She has sent out her maidens, she calls From the tops of the heights of the city:
NASB.4: 'Whoever is naive, let him turn in here!' To him who lacks understanding she says,
NASB.5: 'Come, eat of my food And drink of the wine I have mixed.
NASB.6: 'Forsake [your] folly and live, And proceed in the way of understanding.'
NASB.7: He who corrects a scoffer gets dishonor for himself, And he who reproves a wicked man [gets] insults for himself.
NASB.8: Do not reprove a scoffer, or he will hate you, Reprove a wise man and he will love you.
NASB.9: Give [instruction] to a wise man and he will be still wiser, Teach a righteous man and he will increase [his] learning.
NASB.10: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, And the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
NASB.11: For by me your days will be multiplied, And years of life will be added to you.
NASB.12: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself, And if you scoff, you alone will bear it.
NASB.13: The woman of folly is boisterous, [She is] naive and knows nothing.
NASB.14: She sits at the doorway of her house, On a seat by the high places of the city,
NASB.15: Calling to those who pass by, Who are making their paths straight:
NASB.16: 'Whoever is naive, let him turn in here,' And to him who lacks understanding she says,
NASB.17: 'Stolen water is sweet; And bread [eaten] in secret is pleasant.'
NASB.18: But he does not know that the dead are there, [That] her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

NLT.1:
Wisdom has built her house;
she has carved its seven columns.
NLT.2:
She has prepared a great banquet,
mixed the wines, and set the table.
NLT.3:
She has sent her servants to invite everyone to come.
She calls out from the heights overlooking the city.
NLT.4:
“Come in with me,” she urges the simple.
To those who lack good judgment, she says,
NLT.5:
“Come, eat my food,
and drink the wine I have mixed.
NLT.6:
Leave your simple ways behind, and begin to live;
learn to use good judgment.”
NLT.7:
Anyone who rebukes a mocker will get an insult in return.
Anyone who corrects the wicked will get hurt.
NLT.8:
So don’t bother correcting mockers;
they will only hate you.
But correct the wise,
and they will love you.
NLT.9:
Instruct the wise,
and they will be even wiser.
Teach the righteous,
and they will learn even more.
NLT.10:
Fear of the LORD is the foundation of wisdom.
Knowledge of the Holy One results in good judgment.
NLT.11:
Wisdom will multiply your days
and add years to your life.
NLT.12:
If you become wise, you will be the one to benefit.
If you scorn wisdom, you will be the one to suffer.
NLT.13:
The woman named Folly is brash.
She is ignorant and doesn’t know it.
NLT.14:
She sits in her doorway
on the heights overlooking the city.
NLT.15:
She calls out to men going by
who are minding their own business.
NLT.16:
“Come in with me,” she urges the simple.
To those who lack good judgment, she says,
NLT.17:
“Stolen water is refreshing;
food eaten in secret tastes the best!”
NLT.18:
But little do they know that the dead are there.
Her guests are in the depths of the grave.

WEB.1: Wisdom has built her house. She has carved out her seven pillars.
WEB.2: She has prepared her meat. She has mixed her wine. She has also set her table.
WEB.3: She has sent out her maidens. She cries from the highest places of the city:
WEB.4: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” As for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,
WEB.5: “Come, eat some of my bread, Drink some of the wine which I have mixed!
WEB.6: Leave your simple ways, and live. Walk in the way of understanding.”
WEB.7: He who corrects a mocker invites insult. He who reproves a wicked man invites abuse.
WEB.8: Don’t reprove a scoffer, lest he hate you. Reprove a wise man, and he will love you.
WEB.9: Instruct a wise man, and he will be still wiser. Teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning.
WEB.10: The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom. The knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
WEB.11: For by me your days will be multiplied. The years of your life will be increased.
WEB.12: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself. If you mock, you alone will bear it.
WEB.13: The foolish woman is loud, undisciplined, and knows nothing.
WEB.14: She sits at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,
WEB.15: To call to those who pass by, who go straight on their ways,
WEB.16: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here.” as for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,
WEB.17: “Stolen water is sweet. Food eaten in secret is pleasant.”
WEB.18: But he doesn’t know that the departed spirits are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

She has prepared her meat; ⓕ she has mixed her wine; ⓖ

CSB17.3:
She has sent out her female servants; ⓘ

CSB17.4:
“Whoever is inexperienced, enter here!”
To the one who lacks sense, ⓚ she says,
CSB17.5:
“Come, eat my bread, ⓛ
and drink the wine I have mixed.
CSB17.6:
Leave inexperience behind, and you will live;
pursue the way of understanding.
CSB17.7:
The one who corrects a mocker
will bring abuse on himself;
the one who rebukes the wicked will get hurt. [1]
CSB17.8:
Don’t rebuke a mocker, or he will hate you; ⓜ

CSB17.9:
Instruct the wise, and he will be wiser still;

CSB17.10:
“The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, ⓑ
and the knowledge of the Holy One ⓒ is understanding.

For by me your days will be many,
and years will be added to your life.
CSB17.12:
If you are wise, you are wise for your own benefit; ⓓ
if you mock, you alone will bear the consequences.”
CSB17.13:

she is gullible and knows nothing. ⓕ

She sits by the doorway of her house,
on a seat at the highest point of the city,
CSB17.15:
calling to those who pass by,
who go straight ahead on their paths: ⓖ

“Whoever is inexperienced, enter here!”
To the one who lacks sense, she says,

AMP.1:
Wisdom has built her [spacious and sufficient] house;
She has hewn out and set up her seven pillars.
AMP.2:
She has prepared her food, she has mixed her wine;
She has also set her table.
AMP.3:
She has sent out her maidens, she calls
From the highest places of the city:
AMP.4:
"Whoever is naive or inexperienced, let him turn in here!"
As for him who lacks understanding, she says,
AMP.5:
"Come, eat my food
And drink the wine I have mixed [and accept my gifts].
AMP.6:
"Leave [behind] your foolishness [and the foolish] and live,
And walk in the way of insight and understanding."
AMP.7:
He who corrects and instructs a scoffer gets dishonor for himself,
And he who rebukes a wicked man gets insults for himself.
AMP.8:
Do not correct a scoffer [who foolishly ridicules and takes no responsibility for his error] or he will hate you;
Correct a wise man [who learns from his error], and he will love you.
AMP.9:
Give instruction to a wise man and he will become even wiser;
Teach a righteous man and he will increase his learning.
AMP.10:
The [reverent] fear of the LORD [that is, worshiping Him and regarding Him as truly awesome] is the beginning and the preeminent part of wisdom [its starting point and its essence],
And the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding and spiritual insight.
AMP.11:
For by me (wisdom from God) your days will be multiplied,
And years of life shall be increased.
AMP.12:
If you are wise, you are wise for yourself [for your own benefit];
If you scoff [thoughtlessly ridicule and disdain], you alone will pay the penalty.
AMP.13:
The foolish woman is restless and noisy;
She is naive and easily misled and thoughtless, and knows nothing at all [of eternal value].
AMP.14:
She sits at the doorway of her house,
On a seat by the high and conspicuous places of the city,
AMP.15:
Calling to those who pass by,
Who are making their paths straight:
AMP.16:
"Whoever is naive or inexperienced, let him turn in here!"
And to him who lacks understanding (common sense), she says,
AMP.17:
"Stolen waters (pleasures) are sweet [because they are forbidden];
And bread eaten in secret is pleasant."
AMP.18:
But he does not know that the spirits of the dead are there,
And that her guests are [already] in the depths of Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead).

MSG.1:
Lady Wisdom has built and furnished her home;
it's supported by seven hewn timbers.
MSG.2:
The banquet meal is ready to be served: lamb roasted,
wine poured out, table set with silver and flowers.
MSG.3:
Having dismissed her serving maids,
Lady Wisdom goes to town, stands in a prominent place,
and invites everyone within sound of her voice:
MSG.4:
"Are you confused about life, don't know what's going on?
Come with me, oh come, have dinner with me!
MSG.5:
I've prepared a wonderful spread—fresh-baked bread,
roast lamb, carefully selected wines.
MSG.6:
Leave your impoverished confusion and live!
Walk up the street to a life with meaning."
MSG.7:
If you reason with an arrogant cynic, you'll get slapped in the face;
confront bad behavior and get a kick in the shins.
MSG.8:
So don't waste your time on a scoffer;
all you'll get for your pains is abuse.
But if you correct those who care about life,
that's different—they'll love you for it!
MSG.9:
Save your breath for the wise—they'll be wiser for it;
tell good people what you know—they'll profit from it.
MSG.10:
Skilled living gets its start in the Fear-of-God,
insight into life from knowing a Holy God.
MSG.11:
It's through me, Lady Wisdom, that your life deepens,
and the years of your life ripen.
MSG.12:
Live wisely and wisdom will permeate your life;
mock life and life will mock you.
MSG.13:
Then there's this other woman, Madame Whore—
brazen, empty-headed, frivolous.
MSG.14:
She sits on the front porch
of her house on Main Street,
MSG.15:
And as people walk by minding
their own business, calls out,
MSG.16: "Are you confused about life, don't know what's going on?
MSG.17:
Steal off with me, I'll show you a good time!
No one will ever know—I'll give you the time of your life."
MSG.18:
But they don't know about all the skeletons in her closet,
that all her guests end up in hell.

NIV.1:
Invitations of Wisdom and of Folly
Wisdom has built her house;
she has hewn out its seven pillars.
NIV.2:
She has prepared her meat and mixed her wine;
she has also set her table.
NIV.3:
She has sent out her maids, and she calls
from the highest point of the city.
NIV.4:
“Let all who are simple come in here!”
she says to those who lack judgment.
NIV.5:
“Come, eat my food
and drink the wine I have mixed.
NIV.6:
Leave your simple ways and you will live;
walk in the way of understanding.
NIV.7:
“Whoever corrects a mocker invites insult;
whoever rebukes a wicked man incurs abuse.
NIV.8:
Do not rebuke a mocker or he will hate you;
rebuke a wise man and he will love you.
NIV.9:
Instruct a wise man and he will be wiser still;
teach a righteous man and he will add to his learning.
NIV.10:
“The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom,
and knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
NIV.11:
For through me your days will be many,
and years will be added to your life.
NIV.12:
If you are wise, your wisdom will reward you;
if you are a mocker, you alone will suffer.”
NIV.13:
The woman Folly is loud;
she is undisciplined and without knowledge.
NIV.14:
She sits at the door of her house,
on a seat at the highest point of the city,
NIV.15:
calling out to those who pass by,
who go straight on their way.
NIV.16:
“Let all who are simple come in here!”
she says to those who lack judgment.
NIV.17:
“Stolen water is sweet;
food eaten in secret is delicious!”
NIV.18:
But little do they know that the dead are there,
that her guests are in the depths of the grave.

NRSVCE.1:
Wisdom has built her house,
she has hewn her seven pillars.
NRSVCE.2:
She has slaughtered her animals, she has mixed her wine,
she has also set her table.
NRSVCE.3:
She has sent out her servant-girls, she calls
from the highest places in the town,
NRSVCE.4:
“You that are simple, turn in here!”
To those without sense she says,
NRSVCE.5:
“Come, eat of my bread
and drink of the wine I have mixed.
NRSVCE.6:
Lay aside immaturity, and live,
and walk in the way of insight.”
NRSVCE.7:
Whoever corrects a scoffer wins abuse;
whoever rebukes the wicked gets hurt.
NRSVCE.8:
A scoffer who is rebuked will only hate you;
the wise, when rebuked, will love you.
NRSVCE.9:
Give instruction to the wise, and they will become wiser still;
teach the righteous and they will gain in learning.
NRSVCE.10:
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom,
and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight.
NRSVCE.11:
For by me your days will be multiplied,
and years will be added to your life.
NRSVCE.12:
If you are wise, you are wise for yourself;
if you scoff, you alone will bear it.
NRSVCE.13:
The foolish woman is loud;
she is ignorant and knows nothing.
NRSVCE.14:
She sits at the door of her house,
on a seat at the high places of the town,
NRSVCE.15:
calling to those who pass by,
who are going straight on their way,
NRSVCE.16:
“You who are simple, turn in here!”
And to those without sense she says,
NRSVCE.17:
“Stolen water is sweet,
and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
NRSVCE.18:
But they do not know that the dead are there,
that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

NET.1: Wisdom has built her house; she has carved out its seven pillars.
NET.2: She has prepared her meat, she has mixed her wine; she also has arranged her table.
NET.3: She has sent out her female servants; she calls out on the highest places of the city.
NET.4: “Whoever is naive, let him turn in here,” she says to those who lack understanding.
NET.5: “Come, eat some of my food, and drink some of the wine I have mixed.
NET.6: Abandon your foolish ways so that you may live, and proceed in the way of understanding.”
NET.7: Whoever corrects a mocker is asking for insult; whoever reproves a wicked person receives abuse.
NET.8: Do not reprove a mocker or he will hate you; reprove a wise person and he will love you.
NET.9: Give instruction to a wise person, and he will become wiser still; teach a righteous person and he will add to his learning.
NET.10: The beginning of wisdom is to fear the LORD, and acknowledging the Holy One is understanding.
NET.11: For because of me your days will be many, and years will be added to your life.
NET.12: If you are wise, you are wise to your own advantage, but if you are a mocker, you alone must bear it.
NET.13: The woman called Folly is brash, she is naive and does not know anything.
NET.14: So she sits at the door of her house, on a seat at the highest point of the city,
NET.15: calling out to those who are passing by her in the way, who go straight on their way.
NET.16: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here,” she says to those who lack understanding.
NET.17: “Stolen waters are sweet, and food obtained in secret is pleasant!”
NET.18: But they do not realize that the dead are there, that her guests are in the depths of the grave.
